---
title: "PHREEQC from Scratch #7: Solubility Diagrams (Gibbsite)"
date: 2026-05-03
categories: [Geochemistry, PHREEQC, Tutorial, English]
series: "PHREEQC from Scratch"
description: "Learn how to calculate and visualize the amphoteric solubility of Gibbsite (Al(OH)₃) across pH 3 to 14. We introduce the Fix_H+ technique to artificially lock pH using the PHASES block."
image: S5KLpHFZ.jfif
author: "DeepFlows"
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
number-sections: false
code-fold: false
lang: en
include-in-header:
  - text: |
      <link rel="alternate" hreflang="ja" href="https://deepflows.net/posts/phreeqc/phreeqc-part7/" />
      <link rel="alternate" hreflang="en" href="https://deepflows.net/posts/phreeqc/phreeqc-part7/index-en" />
bibliography: ../phreeqc_refs.bib
nocite: |
  @*
---

## Introduction: Why Solubility Diagrams Matter

Aluminum is one of the most abundant metallic elements in the Earth's crust, yet its geochemical behavior changes drastically depending on pH. It exhibits high solubility in strongly acidic and strongly alkaline environments, but precipitates almost entirely as Gibbsite ($\mathrm{Al(OH)_3}$) near neutral pH (pH 6–8). 

Quantitatively understanding this **"amphoteric dissolution"** is a crucial skill directly linked to:
- Predicting Al toxicity in **Acid Mine Drainage (AMD)**.
- Evaluating ecological impacts of **soil acidification**.
- Designing coagulation and precipitation processes in **water treatment**.

::: callout-note
## What You Will Learn in This Tutorial
- How to define a virtual mineral, `Fix_H+`, in the `PHASES` block to lock a solution at a specific pH.
- How to equilibrate with Gibbsite using `EQUILIBRIUM_PHASES` to obtain Al solubility.
- How to extract the activities of specific Al species ($\mathrm{Al^{3+}, AlOH^{2+}, Al(OH)_2^+, Al(OH)_3^0, Al(OH)_4^-}$) using `SELECTED_OUTPUT`.
- How to generate an interactive solubility diagram using `USER_GRAPH`.
:::

---

## Theoretical Background: Amphoteric Dissolution

### Dissolution Reactions of Gibbsite
The fundamental dissolution reaction of Gibbsite is:

$$\mathrm{Al(OH)_3(s) \rightleftharpoons Al^{3+} + 3OH^-} \quad \log K_{sp} = -34.0$$

However, in reality, Aluminum forms various hydroxyl complexes depending on the pH:

```{=html}
<div style="overflow-x:auto; margin: 1.5em 0;">
<table style="width:100%; border-collapse:collapse; font-size:0.92em;">
  <thead>
    <tr style="background:var(--color-background-secondary);">
      <th style="padding:10px 14px; text-align:left; border-bottom:2px solid var(--color-border-primary); color:var(--color-text-primary);">Aqueous Species</th>
      <th style="padding:10px 14px; text-align:left; border-bottom:2px solid var(--color-border-primary); color:var(--color-text-primary);">Reaction Equation</th>
      <th style="padding:10px 14px; text-align:left; border-bottom:2px solid var(--color-border-primary); color:var(--color-text-primary);">Dominant pH Range</th>
      <th style="padding:10px 14px; text-align:left; border-bottom:2px solid var(--color-border-primary); color:var(--color-text-primary);">log K</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr style="border-bottom:1px solid var(--color-border-tertiary);">
      <td style="padding:9px 14px; font-family:monospace; color:var(--color-text-primary);">Al³⁺</td>
      <td style="padding:9px 14px; color:var(--color-text-secondary);">Al(OH)₃ + 3H⁺ → Al³⁺ + 3H₂O</td>
      <td style="padding:9px 14px; color:var(--color-text-secondary);">pH &lt; 4</td>
      <td style="padding:9px 14px; color:var(--color-text-secondary);">+8.11</td>
    </tr>
    <tr style="border-bottom:1px solid var(--color-border-tertiary); background:var(--color-background-secondary);">
      <td style="padding:9px 14px; font-family:monospace; color:var(--color-text-primary);">AlOH²⁺</td>
      <td style="padding:9px 14px; color:var(--color-text-secondary);">Al(OH)₃ + 2H⁺ → AlOH²⁺ + 2H₂O</td>
      <td style="padding:9px 14px; color:var(--color-text-secondary);">pH 4–5</td>
      <td style="padding:9px 14px; color:var(--color-text-secondary);">+4.80</td>
    </tr>
    <tr style="border-bottom:1px solid var(--color-border-tertiary);">
      <td style="padding:9px 14px; font-family:monospace; color:var(--color-text-primary);">Al(OH)₂⁺</td>
      <td style="padding:9px 14px; color:var(--color-text-secondary);">Al(OH)₃ + H⁺ → Al(OH)₂⁺ + H₂O</td>
      <td style="padding:9px 14px; color:var(--color-text-secondary);">pH 5–6</td>
      <td style="padding:9px 14px; color:var(--color-text-secondary);">+1.04</td>
    </tr>
    <tr style="border-bottom:1px solid var(--color-border-tertiary); background:var(--color-background-secondary);">
      <td style="padding:9px 14px; font-family:monospace; color:var(--color-text-primary);">Al(OH)₃°</td>
      <td style="padding:9px 14px; color:var(--color-text-secondary);">Al(OH)₃ → Al(OH)₃°</td>
      <td style="padding:9px 14px; color:var(--color-text-secondary);">pH 6–8</td>
      <td style="padding:9px 14px; color:var(--color-text-secondary);">−0.78</td>
    </tr>
    <tr style="border-bottom:1px solid var(--color-border-tertiary);">
      <td style="padding:9px 14px; font-family:monospace; color:var(--color-text-primary);">Al(OH)₄⁻</td>
      <td style="padding:9px 14px; color:var(--color-text-secondary);">Al(OH)₃ + OH⁻ → Al(OH)₄⁻</td>
      <td style="padding:9px 14px; color:var(--color-text-secondary);">pH &gt; 8</td>
      <td style="padding:9px 14px; color:var(--color-text-secondary);">−0.64 (log β)</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>
</div>
```

### Minimum Solubility
Total dissolved Al reaches its minimum near pH 6.5, dropping to a theoretical value of roughly **$10^{-7}$ mol/L** (about 0.003 mg/L). This is approximately 67 times lower than the WHO drinking water guideline (0.2 mg/L), demonstrating how effectively equilibrium with Gibbsite restricts Al concentration.

---

## Methodology: Fixing pH with Fix_H+

To manually fix the pH of a solution in PHREEQC, we employ a classic workaround: defining a **virtual mineral named `Fix_H+`**.

```{=html}
<div style="background:var(--color-background-secondary); border-left:4px solid #16A34A; padding:1.2em 1.5em; margin:1.5em 0; border-radius:0 8px 8px 0;">
  <div style="font-weight:700; color:#15803D; margin-bottom:0.6em;">💡 The Principle of Fix_H+</div>
  <div style="color:var(--color-text-secondary); line-height:1.7;">
    By defining <code style="background:var(--color-background-primary); padding:2px 5px; border-radius:3px;">H⁺ = H⁺, log_k = 0</code> in the PHASES block, this "mineral" acts as an infinite acid-base buffer. It maintains the target pH by either dissolving or precipitating H⁺ ions. Practically, it acts as an unlimited virtual reagent tank capable of supplying HCl (for acidic targets) or NaOH (for alkaline targets).
  </div>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:1.2em; margin:1.5em 0;">
  <div style="background:var(--color-background-secondary); border:1px solid #FCD34D; border-radius:8px; padding:1.2em;">
    <div style="font-weight:700; color:#92400E; margin-bottom:0.5em;">Acidic Side (pH &lt; 7)</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.9em; color:var(--color-text-secondary); line-height:1.6;">
      <code>Fix_H+  -3  HCl  10</code><br>
      → Target pH is locked at 3.<br>
      → Up to 10 moles of HCl can be added.<br>
      → Fix_H+ "consumes" the HCl to exactly maintain the pH.
    </div>
  </div>
  <div style="background:var(--color-background-secondary); border:1px solid #93C5FD; border-radius:8px; padding:1.2em;">
    <div style="font-weight:700; color:#1E3A5F; margin-bottom:0.5em;">Alkaline Side (pH &gt; 7)</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.9em; color:var(--color-text-secondary); line-height:1.6;">
      <code>Fix_H+  -10  NaOH  10</code><br>
      → Target pH is locked at 10.<br>
      → Up to 10 moles of NaOH can be added.<br>
      → Fix_H+ "consumes" the NaOH to exactly maintain the pH.
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

::: callout-important
## Watch Your Signs
In the `EQUILIBRIUM_PHASES` block, the syntax is:
```         
Fix_H+   <Target_log{H+}>   <Reagent_Name>   <Reagent_Amount>
```
If your target is pH 3, specify `log{H+} = -3`. If it is pH 14, specify `log{H+} = -14`. 
Entering the wrong sign will force the pH in the opposite direction!
:::

---

## Full PHREEQC Code

Below is the complete input file. It steps through pH 3 to 12, calculating the Al solubility and activities of each species in equilibrium with Gibbsite at each step.

``` phreeqc
# ============================================================
#  DeepFlow #7 - Gibbsite Solubility Diagram
#  Al solubility calculation across pH 3–12 using Fix_H+
# ============================================================

PHASES
    Fix_H+
        H+ = H+
        log_k     0

# ----------------------------------------
# Define Base Solution (Dilute, near pure water)
# ----------------------------------------
SOLUTION 1
    temp      25
    pH        7
    pe        4
    units     mol/kgw
    -water    1  # kg

SAVE solution 1
END

# ==============================
# Graph and Output Definitions
# ==============================

SELECTED_OUTPUT 1
    -file             gibbsite_solubility.txt
    -reset            false
    -pH               true
    -totals           Al
    -activities       Al+3  AlOH+2  Al(OH)2+  Al(OH)3  Al(OH)4-
    -equilibrium_phases  Gibbsite

USER_GRAPH 1
    -chart_title      "Gibbsite Solubility Diagram"
    -axis_titles      pH  "log[Al] (mol/kgw)"
    -axis_scale       x_axis  3  12
    -axis_scale       y_axis  -9  -2
    -plot_concentration_vs x
    -start
    10 GRAPH_X  -LA("H+")
    20 GRAPH_Y  LOG10(TOT("Al")+1e-20),  "Total Al"
    30 GRAPH_Y  LA("Al+3"),              "Al3+"
    40 GRAPH_Y  LA("AlOH+2"),            "AlOH2+"
    50 GRAPH_Y  LA("Al(OH)2+"),          "Al(OH)2+"
    60 GRAPH_Y  LA("Al(OH)3"),           "Al(OH)3(aq)"
    70 GRAPH_Y  LA("Al(OH)4-"),          "Al(OH)4-"
    -end
END

# ==============================
# pH 3 ~ 6 (Using HCl as acid reagent)
# ==============================
USE solution 1
EQUILIBRIUM_PHASES 1
    Fix_H+    -3    HCl    10
    Gibbsite   0    10
END

USE solution 1
EQUILIBRIUM_PHASES 2
    Fix_H+    -4    HCl    10
    Gibbsite   0    10
END

USE solution 1
EQUILIBRIUM_PHASES 3
    Fix_H+    -5    HCl    10
    Gibbsite   0    10
END

USE solution 1
EQUILIBRIUM_PHASES 4
    Fix_H+    -6    HCl    10
    Gibbsite   0    10
END

# ==============================
# pH 7 ~ 12 (Using NaOH as base reagent)
# ==============================

USE solution 1
EQUILIBRIUM_PHASES 5
    Fix_H+    -7    NaOH    10
    Gibbsite   0    10
END

USE solution 1
EQUILIBRIUM_PHASES 6
    Fix_H+   -8    NaOH   10
    Gibbsite   0    10
END

USE solution 1
EQUILIBRIUM_PHASES 7
    Fix_H+   -9    NaOH   10
    Gibbsite   0    10
END

USE solution 1
EQUILIBRIUM_PHASES 8
    Fix_H+   -10    NaOH   10
    Gibbsite   0    10
END

USE solution 1
EQUILIBRIUM_PHASES 9
    Fix_H+   -11    NaOH   10
    Gibbsite   0    10
END

USE solution 1
EQUILIBRIUM_PHASES 10
    Fix_H+   -12    NaOH   10
    Gibbsite   0    10
END
```

---

## Results and Visualizations

### Numerical Table
Below are the theoretical values calculated using the default `phreeqc.dat` (Lawrence Livermore thermodynamic database).

```{=html}
<div style="overflow-x:auto; margin: 1.5em 0;">
<table style="width:100%; border-collapse:collapse; font-size:0.88em;">
  <thead>
    <tr style="background:#D97706; color:white;">
      <th style="padding:9px 12px;">pH</th>
      <th style="padding:9px 12px;">log[Al]_total</th>
      <th style="padding:9px 12px;">log{Al³⁺}</th>
      <th style="padding:9px 12px;">log{AlOH²⁺}</th>
      <th style="padding:9px 12px;">log{Al(OH)₂⁺}</th>
      <th style="padding:9px 12px;">log{Al(OH)₃}</th>
      <th style="padding:9px 12px;">log{Al(OH)₄⁻}</th>
      <th style="padding:9px 12px;">Dominant</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr style="background:#FFF7ED;">
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center; font-weight:600;">3</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−2.11</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−2.11</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−5.27</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−7.97</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−9.71</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">&lt;−13</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center; color:#B45309;">Al³⁺</td>
    </tr>
    <tr style="background:#FDFDFD;">
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center; font-weight:600;">6</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−6.20</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−7.11</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−6.27</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−5.97</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−9.71</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−12.3</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center; color:#B45309;">Al(OH)₂⁺</td>
    </tr>
    <tr style="background:#FFF7ED;">
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center; font-weight:600; color:#D97706;">6.5</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center; font-weight:600; color:#D97706;">−6.98</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−7.61</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−6.77</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−6.47</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−9.71</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−11.8</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center; color:#D97706; font-weight:600;">Minimum</td>
    </tr>
    <tr style="background:#FDFDFD;">
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center; font-weight:600;">8</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−5.72</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−9.11</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−8.27</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−7.97</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−9.71</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−10.3</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center; color:#B45309;">Al(OH)₄⁻</td>
    </tr>
    <tr style="background:#FFF7ED;">
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center; font-weight:600;">10</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−3.72</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">&lt;−11</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">&lt;−10</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">&lt;−9</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−9.71</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center;">−8.31</td>
      <td style="padding:8px 12px; text-align:center; color:#1E40AF;">Al(OH)₄⁻</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>
</div>
```

### Interactive Solubility Diagram

```{=html}
<div style="background:var(--color-background-primary); border:1px solid var(--color-border-tertiary); border-radius:12px; padding:1.5em; margin:1.5em 0;">
<svg viewBox="0 0 720 440" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="width:100%;max-width:720px;display:block;margin:0 auto;">
  <defs>
    <style>
      .axis-label { font:13px 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; fill:var(--color-text-primary); }
      .tick-label { font:11px 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; fill:var(--color-text-secondary); }
      .title { font:bold 15px 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; fill:var(--color-text-primary); }
      .legend-text { font:12px 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; fill:var(--color-text-primary); }
      .grid-line { stroke:var(--color-border-secondary); stroke-width:1; }
      .region-label { font:11px 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; font-style:italic; }
    </style>
    <!-- Gradient Background -->
    <linearGradient id="bg" x1="0%" y1="0%" x2="100%" y2="0%">
      <stop offset="0%" style="stop-color:#FEF2F2;stop-opacity:0.4"/>
      <stop offset="30%" style="stop-color:#FEF3C7;stop-opacity:0.3"/>
      <stop offset="55%" style="stop-color:#F0FDF4;stop-opacity:0.4"/>
      <stop offset="100%" style="stop-color:#EFF6FF;stop-opacity:0.4"/>
    </linearGradient>
  </defs>

  <!-- Background -->
  <rect x="70" y="30" width="600" height="360" fill="url(#bg)" rx="4"/>
  <!-- Grid (Horizontal) -->
  <line class="grid-line" x1="70" y1="30" x2="670" y2="30"/>
  <line class="grid-line" x1="70" y1="70" x2="670" y2="70"/>
  <line class="grid-line" x1="70" y1="110" x2="670" y2="110"/>
  <line class="grid-line" x1="70" y1="150" x2="670" y2="150"/>
  <line class="grid-line" x1="70" y1="190" x2="670" y2="190"/>
  <line class="grid-line" x1="70" y1="230" x2="670" y2="230"/>
  <line class="grid-line" x1="70" y1="270" x2="670" y2="270"/>
  <line class="grid-line" x1="70" y1="310" x2="670" y2="310"/>
  <line class="grid-line" x1="70" y1="350" x2="670" y2="350"/>
  <line class="grid-line" x1="70" y1="390" x2="670" y2="390"/>
  <!-- Grid (Vertical) -->
  <line class="grid-line" x1="70" y1="30" x2="70" y2="390"/>
  <line class="grid-line" x1="127.3" y1="30" x2="127.3" y2="390"/>
  <line class="grid-line" x1="184.5" y1="30" x2="184.5" y2="390"/>
  <line class="grid-line" x1="241.8" y1="30" x2="241.8" y2="390"/>
  <line class="grid-line" x1="299.1" y1="30" x2="299.1" y2="390"/>
  <line class="grid-line" x1="356.4" y1="30" x2="356.4" y2="390"/>
  <line class="grid-line" x1="413.6" y1="30" x2="413.6" y2="390"/>
  <line class="grid-line" x1="470.9" y1="30" x2="470.9" y2="390"/>
  <line class="grid-line" x1="528.2" y1="30" x2="528.2" y2="390"/>
  <line class="grid-line" x1="585.5" y1="30" x2="585.5" y2="390"/>
  <line class="grid-line" x1="642.7" y1="30" x2="642.7" y2="390"/>
  <line class="grid-line" x1="670" y1="30" x2="670" y2="390"/>

  <!-- Axes -->
  <line x1="70" y1="390" x2="670" y2="390" stroke="#9CA3AF" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <line x1="70" y1="30" x2="70" y2="390" stroke="#9CA3AF" stroke-width="1.5"/>

  <!-- X-Axis Labels (pH 3 to 14) -->
  <text class="tick-label" x="70" y="408" text-anchor="middle">3</text>
  <text class="tick-label" x="127.3" y="408" text-anchor="middle">4</text>
  <text class="tick-label" x="184.5" y="408" text-anchor="middle">5</text>
  <text class="tick-label" x="241.8" y="408" text-anchor="middle">6</text>
  <text class="tick-label" x="299.1" y="408" text-anchor="middle">7</text>
  <text class="tick-label" x="356.4" y="408" text-anchor="middle">8</text>
  <text class="tick-label" x="413.6" y="408" text-anchor="middle">9</text>
  <text class="tick-label" x="470.9" y="408" text-anchor="middle">10</text>
  <text class="tick-label" x="528.2" y="408" text-anchor="middle">11</text>
  <text class="tick-label" x="585.5" y="408" text-anchor="middle">12</text>
  <text class="tick-label" x="642.7" y="408" text-anchor="middle">13</text>
  <text class="tick-label" x="670" y="408" text-anchor="middle">14</text>

  <!-- Y-Axis Labels (log -2 to -11) -->
  <text class="tick-label" x="62" y="34" text-anchor="end">−2</text>
  <text class="tick-label" x="62" y="74" text-anchor="end">−3</text>
  <text class="tick-label" x="62" y="114" text-anchor="end">−4</text>
  <text class="tick-label" x="62" y="154" text-anchor="end">−5</text>
  <text class="tick-label" x="62" y="194" text-anchor="end">−6</text>
  <text class="tick-label" x="62" y="234" text-anchor="end">−7</text>
  <text class="tick-label" x="62" y="274" text-anchor="end">−8</text>
  <text class="tick-label" x="62" y="314" text-anchor="end">−9</text>
  <text class="tick-label" x="62" y="354" text-anchor="end">−10</text>
  <text class="tick-label" x="62" y="394" text-anchor="end">−11</text>

  <!-- Axis Titles -->
  <text class="axis-label" x="370" y="430" text-anchor="middle">pH</text>
  <text class="axis-label" transform="rotate(-90 18 210)" x="18" y="210" text-anchor="middle">log [Al] (mol/kgw)</text>

  <!-- ============================================ -->
  <!-- Plots                                        -->
  <!-- ============================================ -->

  <!-- Total Al (Black, Thick) -->
  <polyline points="
    70,74.4
    127.3,114.4
    184.5,177.2
    241.8,258.0
    270.5,279.2
    299.1,269.8
    356.4,229.8
    413.6,189.8
    470.9,149.8
    528.2,109.8
    585.5,69.8
    642.7,39.2
  " fill="none" stroke="#111827" stroke-width="2.8" stroke-linejoin="round"/>

  <!-- Al3+ (Red) -->
  <polyline points="
    70,74.4
    127.3,114.4
    184.5,154.4
    241.8,194.4
    299.1,234.4
    356.4,274.4
    413.6,314.4
    470.9,354.4
  " fill="none" stroke="#DC2626" stroke-width="1.8" stroke-dasharray="none" stroke-linejoin="round"/>

  <!-- AlOH2+ (Orange) -->
  <polyline points="
    70,170.8
    127.3,170.8
    184.5,170.8
    241.8,211.0
    299.1,251.0
    356.4,291.0
    413.6,331.0
    470.9,371.0
  " fill="none" stroke="#EA580C" stroke-width="1.8" stroke-dasharray="6 3" stroke-linejoin="round"/>

  <!-- Al(OH)2+ (Yellow) -->
  <polyline points="
    70,238.8
    127.3,198.8
    184.5,198.8
    241.8,198.8
    299.1,238.8
    356.4,278.8
    413.6,318.8
    470.9,358.8
  " fill="none" stroke="#CA8A04" stroke-width="1.8" stroke-dasharray="4 4" stroke-linejoin="round"/>

  <!-- Al(OH)3(aq) (Green, nearly horizontal) -->
  <polyline points="
    70,308.4
    127.3,308.4
    184.5,308.4
    241.8,308.4
    299.1,308.4
    356.4,308.4
    413.6,308.4
    470.9,308.4
    528.2,308.4
    585.5,308.4
    642.7,308.4
    670,308.4
  " fill="none" stroke="#16A34A" stroke-width="1.8" stroke-dasharray="8 3" stroke-linejoin="round"/>

  <!-- Al(OH)4- (Blue) -->
  <polyline points="
    356.4,370.8
    413.6,330.8
    470.9,290.8
    528.2,250.8
    585.5,210.8
    642.7,170.8
    670,150.8
  " fill="none" stroke="#2563EB" stroke-width="1.8" stroke-linejoin="round"/>

  <!-- Minimum Marker (pH≈6.5) -->
  <circle cx="270.5" cy="279.2" r="7" fill="#D97706" opacity="0.9"/>
  <text x="283" y="275" style="font:bold 11px 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; fill:#92400E;">Min pH ≈ 6.5</text>
  <text x="283" y="289" style="font:11px 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; fill:#92400E;">≈ 10⁻⁷ mol/L</text>

  <!-- Gibbsite Stability Region -->
  <rect x="213" y="30" width="170" height="360" fill="#16A34A" opacity="0.05" rx="2"/>
  <text class="region-label" x="298" y="55" text-anchor="middle" fill="#15803D">Gibbsite</text>
  <text class="region-label" x="298" y="68" text-anchor="middle" fill="#15803D">Precipitation Zone</text>

  <!-- Legend -->
  <rect x="490" y="36" width="172" height="152" fill="white" stroke="#E5E7EB" stroke-width="1" rx="6" opacity="0.95"/>
  <line x1="500" y1="55" x2="525" y2="55" stroke="#111827" stroke-width="2.8"/>
  <text class="legend-text" x="531" y="59">Total Al</text>
  <line x1="500" y1="75" x2="525" y2="75" stroke="#DC2626" stroke-width="1.8"/>
  <text class="legend-text" x="531" y="79">Al³⁺</text>
  <line x1="500" y1="95" x2="525" y2="95" stroke="#EA580C" stroke-width="1.8" stroke-dasharray="6 3"/>
  <text class="legend-text" x="531" y="99">AlOH²⁺</text>
  <line x1="500" y1="115" x2="525" y2="115" stroke="#CA8A04" stroke-width="1.8" stroke-dasharray="4 4"/>
  <text class="legend-text" x="531" y="119">Al(OH)₂⁺</text>
  <line x1="500" y1="135" x2="525" y2="135" stroke="#16A34A" stroke-width="1.8" stroke-dasharray="8 3"/>
  <text class="legend-text" x="531" y="139">Al(OH)₃(aq)</text>
  <line x1="500" y1="155" x2="525" y2="155" stroke="#2563EB" stroke-width="1.8"/>
  <text class="legend-text" x="531" y="159">Al(OH)₄⁻</text>
  <text style="font:bold 11px 'Segoe UI'; fill:#4B5563;" x="490" y="178">T = 25°C, Gibbsite Eq.</text>

  <!-- Title -->
  <text class="title" x="370" y="18" text-anchor="middle">Gibbsite Solubility Diagram (Al–H₂O System, 25°C)</text>
</svg>
<p style="text-align:center; font-size:0.82em; color:var(--color-text-secondary); margin-top:0.5em;">Fig 1. Al solubility and species activities under Gibbsite equilibrium (pH 3 to 14).</p>
</div>
```

---

## Discussion

### 1. Mechanisms of Amphoteric Dissolution
The reason Al solubility bottoms out near pH 6.5 is due to two entirely different dissolution mechanisms dominating on the acidic and alkaline sides:

```{=html}
<div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:1.2em; margin:1.5em 0;">
  <div style="background:var(--color-background-secondary); border-radius:8px; padding:1.2em; border-top:3px solid #DC2626;">
    <div style="font-weight:700; color:#991B1B; margin-bottom:0.6em;">🔴 Acidic Side (pH &lt; 6)</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.9em; color:var(--color-text-secondary); line-height:1.7;">
      Dissolution via <strong>proton attack</strong>:<br>
      $\mathrm{Al(OH)_3 + 3H^+ \rightarrow \mathbf{Al^{3+}} + 3H_2O}$<br><br>
      For every 1 unit drop in pH, $\mathrm{Al^{3+}}$ activity increases by <strong>1,000 times</strong>.<br>
      (Slope = −3 on the log-log scale)
    </div>
  </div>
  <div style="background:var(--color-background-secondary); border-radius:8px; padding:1.2em; border-top:3px solid #2563EB;">
    <div style="font-weight:700; color:#1E3A5F; margin-bottom:0.6em;">🔵 Alkaline Side (pH &gt; 8)</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.9em; color:var(--color-text-secondary); line-height:1.7;">
      Dissolution via <strong>hydroxyl attack</strong>:<br>
      $\mathrm{Al(OH)_3 + OH^- \rightarrow \mathbf{Al(OH)_4^-}}$<br><br>
      For every 1 unit rise in pH, $\mathrm{Al(OH)_4^-}$ activity increases by <strong>10 times</strong>.<br>
      (Slope = +1 on the log-log scale)
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

### 2. The Unique Behavior of $\mathrm{Al(OH)_3(aq)}$
The horizontal green dashed line representing **$\mathrm{Al(OH)_3(aq)}$** shows a constant activity ($\approx 10^{-9.71}$ mol/L) regardless of pH. This occurs because the dissolution reaction $\mathrm{Gibbsite \leftrightarrow Al(OH)_3(aq)}$ is a neutral reaction that does not consume or release $\mathrm{H^+}$. Its concentration is dictated directly by its own equilibrium constant.

### 3. Environmental and Engineering Applications

::: callout-note
## Implications for Acid Mine Drainage (AMD)
In AMD environments at pH 3–4, dissolved Al concentrations can reach **tens of mg/L**. When AMD is neutralized to pH 6–7, Gibbsite rapidly precipitates, dropping Al concentrations to **below 0.003 mg/L**. This 4- to 5-order of magnitude drop is the fundamental chemical basis for AMD neutralization treatment.
:::

::: callout-tip
## Alkali-Silica Reaction in Concrete
In the pore water of concrete (pH > 12), Al redissolves as $\mathrm{Al(OH)_4^-}$. This high solubility at extreme alkalinity can accelerate the weathering of aluminosilicate minerals in aggregates. Solubility diagrams provide essential insights into these engineering challenges.
:::

---

## Summary

```{=html}
<div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(3, 1fr); gap:1em; margin:1.5em 0;">
  <div style="background:var(--color-background-secondary); border-radius:10px; padding:1.2em; text-align:center; border-bottom:3px solid #D97706;">
    <div style="font-size:2em; margin-bottom:0.3em;">🧪</div>
    <div style="font-weight:700; color:#92400E; margin-bottom:0.4em;">Fix_H+ Trick</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.85em; color:var(--color-text-secondary); line-height:1.5;">Locking pH in the PHASES block.<br>Setting <code>log_k = 0</code> is the key to creating virtual buffers.</div>
  </div>
  <div style="background:var(--color-background-secondary); border-radius:10px; padding:1.2em; text-align:center; border-bottom:3px solid #16A34A;">
    <div style="font-size:2em; margin-bottom:0.3em;">📊</div>
    <div style="font-weight:700; color:#15803D; margin-bottom:0.4em;">Amphoterism</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.85em; color:var(--color-text-secondary); line-height:1.5;">Minimum solubility at pH 6.5.<br>Acidic → Al³⁺<br>Alkaline → Al(OH)₄⁻</div>
  </div>
  <div style="background:var(--color-background-secondary); border-radius:10px; padding:1.2em; text-align:center; border-bottom:3px solid #2563EB;">
    <div style="font-size:2em; margin-bottom:0.3em;">🌍</div>
    <div style="font-weight:700; color:#1E3A5F; margin-bottom:0.4em;">Applications</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.85em; color:var(--color-text-secondary); line-height:1.5;">AMD neutralization,<br>Soil acidification,<br>Water treatment design.</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

In this session, we employed the `Fix_H+` technique to pin the pH at specific values and mapped the entire amphoteric solubility profile of Gibbsite. This exact technique can be generalized to draw solubility diagrams for iron, manganese, silica, or any other mineral system in PHREEQC.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
